title: Firoz Shah palace complex
tagline: Firoz Shah palace complex — heritage city of Haryana

overview.short: Hisar-e-Firoza fort city with rampart (including the protected archaeological Firoz Shah palace complex, Lat-Ki-Masjid Complex with an Ashokan pillar) along with the Gujari Mahal a
overview.description: Hisar-e-Firoza fort city with rampart (including the protected archaeological Firoz Shah palace complex, Lat-Ki-Masjid Complex with an Ashokan pillar) along with the Gujari Mahal and Charbagh Garden outside the rampart, located in the modern-day Hisar in Haryana state of India, was built by Firoz Shah Tughlaq of the Delhi Sultanate in 1354 CE on the Sindhu-Saraswati Valley Civilisation (SSVC) and Vedic Period archeological mound of ancient Isukāra city. It is maintained by the Archaeological Survey of India. The narrative that Firoz Shah Tughlaq founded a city in a barren wasteland is contradi

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Blue Bird Lake
topPlaces.description: Blue Bird Lake, Hisar, is a resident and endangered migratory bird wetland habitat, lake and recreation area in the town of Hisar, in the Hisar district of Haryana State, India. Blue Bird Lake is close to Hisar Airport on NH-9 in Hisar, Haryana.

topPlaces.name: Mahabir Stadium
topPlaces.description: Mahavir Stadium, built in 1972, is a multipurpose sports complex located in Hisar city of Haryana, India. It has second largest capacity in Haryana, after the Shah Satnam Ji Cricket Stadium in Sirsa which has capacity of 35,000 spectators.

topPlaces.name: St. Thomas Church, Hisar
topPlaces.description: St. Thomas' Church is located in Hisar city of Haryana, India.

topPlaces.name: Pranpir Badshah tomb
topPlaces.description: Pranpir Badshah's tomb (Hindi: बाबा प्राणपिर बादशाह), built from white material, near Mahabir Stadium in Hisar city of Haryana state in India, is claimed to be a 14th-century tomb of foreign-origin Islamic invader who as ghazi (title for Muslim warrior who kill non-Muslims in Ghazwatul-Hind furtherance of Islam) massacred native Indians, but the tomb itself, site and materials are originally of Hindu origin. There are social and legal campaigns to reclaim it as the Hindu temple, but hindered by the colonial mindset and antiquated laws of India which discriminate against the Indian-origin native religions in favour of foreign-origin religions of invaders and colonisers.

topPlaces.name: Government Polytechnic, Hisar
topPlaces.description: Government Polytechnic, Hisar is a public funded college located in Hisar in the Indian state of Haryana.

topPlaces.name: Old Courts Commercial Complex, Hisar
topPlaces.description: Old Court Commercial Complex is a commercial suburb located in Hisar city and Hisar Vidhan Sabha constituency of Haryana, India. The planning of this area is administered by Haryana Urban Development Authority (HUDA).

items.note: Blue Bird Lake, Hisar, is a resident and endangered migratory bird wetland habitat, lake and recreation area in the town of Hisar, in the Hisar district of Haryana State, India. Blue Bird Lake is close to Hisar Airport on NH-9 in Hisar, Haryana.

Mahavir Stadium, built in 1972, is a multipurpose sports complex located in Hisar city of Haryana, India. It has second largest capacity in Haryana, after the Shah Satnam Ji Cricket Stadium in Sirsa which has capacity of 35,000 spectators.
St. Thomas' Church is located in Hisar city of Haryana, India.

Pranpir Badshah's tomb (Hindi: बाबा प्राणपिर बादशाह), built from white material, near Mahabir Stadium in Hisar city of Haryana state in India, is claimed to be a 14th-century tomb of foreign-origin Islamic invader who as ghazi (title for Muslim warrior who kill non-Muslims in Ghazwatul-Hind furtherance of Islam) massacred native Indians, but the tomb itself, site and materials are originally of Hindu origin. There are social and legal campaigns to reclaim it as the Hindu temple, but hindered by the colonial mindset and antiquated laws of India which discriminate against the Indian-origin native religions in favour of foreign-origin religions of invaders and colonisers.
Government Polytechnic, Hisar is a public funded college located in Hisar in the Indian state of Haryana.

Old Court Commercial Complex is a commercial suburb located in Hisar city and Hisar Vidhan Sabha constituency of Haryana, India. The planning of this area is administered by Haryana Urban Development Authority (HUDA).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Firoz Shah palace complex?
faq.a: The best time to visit Firoz Shah palace complex is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Firoz Shah palace complex?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (151 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (157 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Firoz Shah palace complex?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Firoz Shah palace complex at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Firoz Shah palace complex?
faq.a: Stays in Firoz Shah palace complex start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Firoz Shah palace complex?
faq.a: The top attractions are Blue Bird Lake, Mahabir Stadium, St. Thomas Church, Hisar, Pranpir Badshah tomb, Government Polytechnic, Hisar.
